covariates.label: US isolation at the UN
covariates.note: US isolation at the UN — the world-mean ideal-point distance of UN members from the US in roll-call votes (Voeten UN-voting data). Higher = the US more isolated. Hollow points mark thin vote-years (few US roll-calls) and are excluded from the correlation.

covariates.label: China–US confrontation (GDELT)
covariates.note: Share of US–China interaction events coded conflictual in GDELT (the Global Database of Events, Language & Tone), annual mean weighted by event volume. Higher = more confrontation. Machine-coded from world news; descriptive.

covariates.label: China's relative power (GDP)
covariates.note: China's GDP as a percentage of US GDP (World Bank, current US$). A structural covariate that rises monotonically — a correlation with order-talk volume largely reflects a shared upward trend.

covariates.label: US tariffs on Chinese goods
covariates.axis: Average US tariff on Chinese goods (%)
covariates.note: Average US tariff rate on imports from China (PIIE / Chad Bown's US–China tariff tracker), annual mean. Steps up with the 2018– trade war and again sharply in 2025.
